aleabit.layer_label: Layer 3 — AI server liquid cooling / data center thermal
aleabit.thesis: Pure-play liquid cooling at sub-$2B mcap with a wind-power-converter cooling pedigree that they're pivoting into AI data center cold-plate and CDU work. The chokepoint is real — thermal density in 100kW+ racks is the actual bottleneck behind H20/Ascend deployment in China — but Gaolan is the smaller second mover behind Yingweike, and the AI revenue mix is still single digits.

signals.name: 供应链汇聚
signals.note: Cooling vendors for Chinese hyperscaler AI racks narrow to ~5 names

signals.name: 材料价格 ATH
signals.note: Copper cold plates, not exotic materials

signals.name: 分析师覆盖率 < 3
signals.note: Small-cap, thin coverage outside theme-trading desks

signals.name: 技术深度门槛
signals.note: Liquid-cooling reliability/leak testing is non-trivial but not Layer-4 hard

signals.name: 政府/国防关联
signals.note: Civilian DC and EV converter cooling

signals.name: CEO 二级市场买入
signals.note: No public open-market insider buy on record

signals.name: 小盘 + 关键产能
signals.note: ~RMB 13.9B mcap sits in the sweet spot

red_flags: PB ~10 is rich for a transition story
Concentration risk on a few hyperscaler customers
Yingweike eats most of the narrative
aleabit.ai_relevance: Direct — liquid cooling is the thermal chokepoint for >50kW AI racks; secondary supplier into domestic AI server OEMs.
